title: QuickAdapt Universal Task
description: Normalized representation of a task/to-do/ticket from any project management platform. This is the intermediate format that all source adapters must output. It preserves structured data before transformation into the Glean Indexing API payload. All nested structures are designed to flatten cleanly into Glean's document model (body.textContent, customProperties, and allowedUsers/Groups).

source.description: Identifies where this task came from. Critical for traceability — when something looks wrong in Glean, you need to trace it back to the originating platform and record.

platform.description: Lowercase identifier for the source platform (e.g., 'asana', 'clickup', 'basecamp'). Used to route back to the correct adapter if re-syncing is needed.

platformTaskId.description: The task's native ID in the source platform. Preserved exactly as-is — no transformation. This is your audit trail.

id.description: QuickAdapt's normalized ID. Alphanumeric only — this is a Glean constraint. Generated by combining the platform name and native ID (e.g., 'asana' + '1234567' = 'asana1234567'). This ensures uniqueness across platforms while conforming to Glean's ID restrictions.

objectType.description: Distinguishes what kind of work item this is within the datasource. Maps directly to Glean's 'objectType' on the document definition. Common values: 'task', 'subtask', 'milestone', 'bug', 'epic', 'story'. Adapters should normalize platform-specific terminology (e.g., Basecamp 'to-do' → 'task', Jira 'issue' with type 'Bug' → 'bug').

title.description: The task title. Maps directly to Glean's document 'title' field. Every platform has this — it's the most universal field.

description.description: The task body/description. Will become part of Glean's 'body.textContent' — this is what makes the task searchable. Some platforms use rich text (HTML/markdown); adapters should strip to plain text.

url.description: Direct link to the task in the source platform. Maps to Glean's 'viewURL'. When someone finds this task in Glean search, this is where they click through to.

status.description: Current task status, lowercased and normalized. Each adapter maps platform-specific statuses to a common set: 'open', 'in_progress', 'review', 'completed', 'cancelled', 'unknown'. This becomes a Glean customProperty for filtering.

priority.description: Task priority, normalized to: 'critical', 'high', 'medium', 'low', 'none'. Becomes a Glean customProperty. Not all platforms have this — 'none' is the safe default.

creator.description: Who created this task. Distinct from assignees — in Jira this is the 'reporter', in Asana it's the 'created_by'. Maps to Glean's 'author' field on the document. Email is the key — Glean uses it for identity resolution.

email.description: Email address. This is how Glean maps this person to an identity in its knowledge graph.
Email address for Glean identity resolution.

assignees.description: People responsible for this task. Array because Asana, ClickUp, Monday, and others all support multiple assignees. The converter flattens this for Glean: first assignee becomes the primary (used in Glean's 'owner' if supported), all assignee names/emails get concatenated into body text and added as customProperties so they remain searchable and filterable.

container.description: The parent context this task lives in. Replaces the old flat 'project' string — now carries an ID for Glean's container relationship mapping. The converter flattens this: 'containerName' becomes a customProperty, 'containerId' is used if the converter builds Glean container documents.

containerId.description: Normalized ID of the parent container (same alphanumeric rules as task ID). Empty string if the platform doesn't expose a usable project/space ID.

containerName.description: Display name of the parent project/list/space/board. What was previously the flat 'project' field. Naming varies wildly across platforms — we normalize the field name, not the value.

containerType.description: What the source platform calls this container. Common values: 'project', 'space', 'board', 'todolist', 'folder'. Stored as a customProperty — useful for filtering when multiple platform types coexist in one Glean datasource.

parentTaskId.description: If this is a subtask, this holds the QuickAdapt normalized ID of the parent task (same format as 'id'). Enables hierarchy reconstruction. The converter can use this to build Glean's document-to-document relationships or simply flatten it as a customProperty for searchability. Null for top-level tasks.

tags.description: Labels/tags attached to the task. The converter concatenates these into body text for searchability in Glean (Glean doesn't have a native tags field) AND sets them as a multi-value customProperty for faceted filtering.

author.description: Display name of the commenter. Email not always available on comments — name is the reliable field.

text.description: Comment body, plain text. Adapters strip HTML/markdown. Rich content (images, attachments) is dropped — text only.

createdAt.description: When the comment was posted. ISO 8601.

comments.description: Task discussion thread. This is often where the real context lives — decisions, blockers, links, status updates. The converter flattens the entire array into a single text block appended to Glean's body.textContent (formatted as 'author (date): text' per entry). Comment count also becomes a customProperty for relevance signals.

key.description: Field name, lowercased and slugified by the adapter (e.g., 'sprint_number', 'client_name', 'story_points'). Must be safe for use as a Glean customProperty key.

value.description: Field value, stringified. Numbers, dates, enums — everything comes through as a string. The converter passes these directly as Glean customProperties. String type keeps the schema simple and avoids type-negotiation between platforms.

customFields.description: Catch-all for platform-specific metadata that doesn't map to any standard field above. Sprint numbers, story points, client names, effort estimates, custom dropdowns — whatever teams have configured. Each entry becomes a Glean customProperty key-value pair. This is what prevents information loss when a platform has fields we didn't anticipate.

permissions.description: Access control for this task. Glean's Indexing API requires ACL definitions on every document — without this, the converter must either make everything globally visible (security risk) or globally restricted (useless). Adapters extract what they can from the source platform; the converter maps this to Glean's allowedUsers/allowedGroups or falls back to datasource-level defaults.

visibility.description: Quick classifier. 'public' = visible to entire org (converter sets allowAllDatasourceUsersAccess in Glean). 'private' = only explicitly listed users. 'restricted' = only listed users and groups. Default is 'restricted' because failing closed is safer than failing open.

allowedUsers.description: Email addresses of users who can see this task. Maps directly to Glean's 'permissions.allowedUsers'. Emails are the identity key — Glean resolves them against its user directory.

allowedGroups.description: Group identifiers (team names, group emails, org-unit IDs) that have access. Maps to Glean's 'permissions.allowedGroups'. What counts as a 'group' varies by platform — adapters normalize to whatever the org uses in Glean's directory.

dueDate.description: ISO 8601 due date. Becomes a Glean customProperty. Nullable — not all tasks have deadlines.

createdAt.description: When the task was created in the source platform. ISO 8601. Maps to Glean's 'createdAt' document timestamp.

updatedAt.description: When the task was last modified in the source platform. ISO 8601. Maps to Glean's 'updatedAt'. Used to determine if a re-sync is needed.

syncedAt.description: When QuickAdapt last pulled this task. This is OUR timestamp, not the source platform's. Critical for debugging sync issues — 'when did we last look at this?'
